shape_semantics: Joint-necessity. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,L,W,_ : ONLY value_profile_cmplog resolves; cmplog blocks AND value_profile blocks; naive is NON-decisive. The vpc-only resolution asserts BOTH I2S and the CMP_MAP gradient are jointly required. Every branch carries TWO Pass-A signatures of ONE joint mechanism (an h0 I2S half + an h1 gradient half), except harfbuzz/7170 which the classifier kept as a single joint signature. Layer 1 already asserts jointness; the evidence test does NOT re-confirm it (that would violate G2). The test discriminates WHICH joint SUBTYPE each branch is, on a measurement INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern: it reads the structural/value content of the cmplog-BLOCKING and value_profile-BLOCKING seed sets and asks what the gradient adds OVER I2S-only and what I2S adds OVER gradient-only.

hypotheses.label: vpc assembles a LARGER, multi-field structure than I2S-only (cmplog) ever reaches; the gradient is necessary to grow size/token-count past where exact substitution stalls, and I2S is necessary because the gradient alone (value_profile) places ~0 structural tokens
hypotheses.prediction: On the value_profile-BLOCKING seed set the structural-token count is ~0 (vp_tags ~ 0): the gradient alone cannot place ANY exact multi-byte structural token (FOURCC table tag, ICC colorspace signature, XML markup literal), so I2S is necessary. AND the vpc-RESOLVING median seed is materially BIGGER than the cmplog-BLOCKING median seed (size_lift >= 1.3): the gradient grows the multi-field header/content-model structure past where exact-substitution-only (cmplog) plateaus, so the gradient is necessary. A pure-I2S-substitution mechanism (no assembly depth) predicts size_lift ~ 1.0; a pure-gradient mechanism predicts vp_tags > 0. Only the assembly-depth joint predicts BOTH vp_tags~0 AND size_lift>=1.3.

hypotheses.label: vpc and cmplog reach a SIMILARLY-sized input (size_lift ~ 1), but vpc lands a specific gradient-climbed VALUE (a single exact multi-byte keyword/token) that the cmplog-blocking seeds approach byte-by-byte yet never reach; I2S is still necessary to splice the keyword operand, and the gradient is necessary for the last-mile prefix precision that the structural size proxy is blind to

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT value_distance_reached tool. For the branch's decisive CMP site, take the target operand value from the signature (the exact SQL keyword/identifier token, e.g. 'PRAGMA' = 50 52 41 47 4d 41 for all four sqlite3 branches; operand_width_bytes=6). Compute the MIN byte-distance (Hamming over the operand-width window) between the target value and the value found at the cmp-site offset across (a) the vpc-RESOLVING seed set => vpc_min_distance and (b) the cmplog-BLOCKING seed set => cmp_min_distance. distance_closure_ratio = (cmp_min_distance - vpc_min_distance) / max(cmp_min_distance, 1).
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes ('PRAGMA', 6); cmp-site offset is the input head (per the cards), confirmed via per-role coverage overlay / analysis byte-diff
params.feasibility: All four value-precision members are sqlite3, which HAS an on-disk corpus AND is among the 286 reps with resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds in the DB, so they are scorable.

hypotheses.prediction: Inputs are the SAME size (size_lift ~ 1.0 -- a 'PRAGMA <name>;' statement is no bigger than the SELECT/junk blocking fragments) BUT the vpc-resolving seeds reach a keyword value strictly CLOSER to the exact target token than the cmplog-blocking seeds ever do: cmp_min_distance > 0 (cmplog seeds prefix-collide on a byte or two of 'PRAGMA' but never accrete the full keyword) while vpc_min_distance ~ 0, so distance_closure_ratio is large. A pure-I2S mechanism would predict cmp_min_distance ~ 0 (cmplog HAS I2S; if one substitution sufficed cmplog would resolve, contradicting L). This rules out 'I2S alone is enough' AND, because size_lift~1, rules out assembly-depth.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 1). The decisive divergence is on a DERIVED-INTEGER header/count field reached over a range/inequality gate (gid >= num_glyphs; glyf->get_path success on jointly-consistent offset/length dwords): the literal table tag is PRESENT in BOTH arms (so assembly-depth's vp_tags<0.3 fails) and input size does NOT lift (so size_lift>=1.3 fails), yet vpc resolves because the CMP_MAP gradient hill-climbs the multi-byte NUMERIC offset/count/length fields toward a jointly-consistent in-range configuration that single-shot I2S substitution (cmplog) cannot reach. I2S is still necessary to plant the dispatching tag; the gradient is necessary for the numeric magnitude convergence that neither the size proxy NOR a fixed-literal value-distance can see (the target is a derived integer with no input literal).

params.comment: Reuses value_distance_reached in its NUMERIC mode (the tool already supports 'magnitude gap for inequality gates'). The gate is an inequality/range on a DERIVED INTEGER (gid >= num_glyphs at glyf.hh:195; jointly-consistent offset/length dwords feeding glyf->get_path at hb-ot-font.cc:467). The target is NOT a fixed input literal (operand_literal=null, operand_kind=derived_integer), so this is a genuinely DIFFERENT measurement from joint_value_precision (which needs a fixed multi-byte literal operand): here the tool computes, per arm, the MIN magnitude gap of the wide LE integer header fields (num_glyphs / loca-glyf offset+length dwords at the decisive cmp site) versus the in-range value the parser requires, over the cmplog-BLOCKING vs vpc-RESOLVING seed sets.
params.site_source: decisive cmp site from the h1 (gradient) signature/card: glyf num_glyphs comparison (6858) and table-directory offset/length dwords feeding glyf->get_path (7245); offsets confirmed via per-role coverage overlay / analysis byte-diff (W-vs-L divergent dword offsets, e.g. 0x18-0x1f per the 7245 card).
params.feasibility: Both members are harfbuzz, which HAS an on-disk corpus AND resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds in the DB -> scorable on s4. Numeric mode reads the same seed_set source as the literal mode; only the distance metric (magnitude gap over the derived-integer window) differs, supplied as a per-branch numeric-site param. If the orchestrator finds value_distance_reached's numeric mode cannot anchor a target without an input literal (the in-range value is computed, not present in the input), this hypothesis degrades to decidable:false rather than force-fit -- see notes.

hypotheses.prediction: vp_tags > 0.3 (the typ1/loca tag IS present in the value_profile-blocking seeds too, so assembly-depth's vp_tags<0.3 fails for these branches) AND size_lift < 1.3 (no structural growth -- both arms are similarly-sized font fragments), so BOTH prior joint rules miss. INDEPENDENTLY, on the derived-integer header field the vpc-resolving arm lands a magnitude STRICTLY closer to the in-range configuration than the cmplog-blocking arm: cmp_min_distance > 0 (cmplog plants the tag but its num_glyphs/offset integers stay out of range) while vpc_min_distance is much smaller (the gradient hill-climbs the numeric fields into range), so winner_closer=true and distance_closure_ratio is large. A pure-I2S story predicts cmp_min_distance ~ 0 (cmplog HAS I2S; if planting the tag sufficed it would resolve -- contradicting L). An assembly-depth story predicts size_lift>=1.3. A fixed-literal value-precision story is inapplicable (no input literal to converge on). Only the numeric-header-convergence joint predicts size_lift<1.3 AND vp_tags>0.3 AND a numeric distance gap on the derived-integer field.
hypotheses.rule: size_lift < 1.3 AND vp_tags >= 0.3 AND cmp_min_distance > 0 AND distance_closure_ratio >= 0.5 AND winner_closer == true

hypotheses.label: NON-DISCRIMINABLE (honest inconclusive). The libxml2 automaton/grammar-state branches (if (to == NULL); nsNr != ctxt->nsNr) resolve because vpc reaches a NON-NULL automaton target state via a GRAMMAR-PATH choice (the optional-quantifier OPT content model vs the MULT model; a namespaced empty-element close), not via a bigger seed, a closer fixed literal, or an in-range derived integer. The decisive feature is WHICH parse production the corpus reached -- a non-local grammar-state property. Plus lcms/2674 is a 4-byte ICC FOURCC equality at constant size (a short, chance-prone operand) that failed both size and value-distance rules.

params.why_undecidable: No BUILT measurement discriminates the joint SUBTYPE here without re-measuring the resolve pattern (G2 violation). (1) The libxml2 gates (7296 xmlFAGenerateEpsilonTransition to==NULL; 7311 xmlAutomataNewEpsilon to==NULL; 9361 xmlParseElementStart nsNr!=ctxt->nsNr) hinge on reaching a NON-NULL automaton state via a grammar production choice (OPT vs MULT) -- XML markup literals are present in BOTH W and L seeds (so vp_tags is not ~0 and size barely lifts: assembly_depth misses), the operand is presence_or_nonnull / derived (no fixed multi-byte literal: value_precision and numeric-header modes both miss), and depth_reach at the branch line would just re-detect that vpc reaches the false side, i.e. verify vpc-resolves with vpc-reaches -- a G2 violation, not an independent signal. There is no built tool that reads 'which grammar production the corpus exercised' as a signal independent of the resolve label. (2) lcms/2674: a single 4-byte FOURCC ('prtr','Lab ') at constant input size; value_distance over a 4-byte window is chance-alignment prone (width-bound caveat), and it already failed both prior rules on real data, so no built measurement cleanly separates its subtype. CROSS-SERVER NOTE: these are NOT marked undecidable because their corpora are off-server (libxml2/lcms are sB; they have resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds in the DB) -- they are undecidable because the MECHANISM is non-local/grammar-state with no independent built measurement. The owning server may revisit if a grammar-path or production-coverage descriptor is later built.

hypotheses.prediction: No falsifiable real-data prediction over named metrics is available that is independent of the resolve pattern; these branches are honestly left inconclusive (G3).

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage 'I2S event' descriptor is used (I2S is untagged across all 4 fuzzers).

value_profile treated as a FEEDBACK, not a mutator -- its effect is measured only via corpus admission / what the corpus reaches (size, tokens, value-distance, numeric magnitude), never a lineage gradient event.

No byte_freq_ratio / byte-alignment on these joint gates (W vs L seeds differ in size for assembly_depth; the numeric-header gate compares a derived integer, not a fixed input offset). struct_size/token_count (assembly), Hamming-window value_distance (value-precision), and numeric magnitude gap (header-convergence) are used instead.

on-disk corpora exist only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3: harfbuzz (assembly + numeric-header) and sqlite3 (value-precision) are scorable on s4; libxml2 + lcms are sB-owned and seed_set-sourced from the DB reps -- but their unlabeled members are marked decidable:false on MECHANISM grounds, not corpus-location grounds.
seed-dependent tools read resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds from db/blockers.sqlite (the 286 reps have them).
notes:
ROUND 1 re-design (loop-until-dry). SUPERSET / FREEZE: both confirmed priors are carried forward VERBATIM (joint_assembly_depth rule 'vp_tags<0.3 AND size_lift>=1.3'; joint_value_precision rule 'size_lift<1.3 AND cmp_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5'), with member_hint_branches narrowed to only the branches that validated under them (assembly_depth: the 6 harfbuzz CPAL/COLR/glyf/CFF font-table branches that scored; value_precision: the 4 sqlite3 PRAGMA branches) so the confirmed branches re-validate unchanged. NO prior rule threshold was relaxed.

PRIORS KEPT: joint_assembly_depth, joint_value_precision (both unchanged).

NEW MECHANISM (1, discriminable): joint_numeric_header_convergence for harfbuzz/6858 (gid>=num_glyphs) and harfbuzz/7245 (glyf->get_path on jointly-consistent offset/length dwords). These two failed BOTH priors for a principled reason readable in their cards/signatures: the I2S half plants the tag (typ1/loca) but the tag is ALSO present in the value_profile-blocking seeds (so assembly_depth's vp_tags<0.3 is violated), and the seeds do not grow (so size_lift>=1.3 is violated); the decisive divergence is on DERIVED-INTEGER header fields (num_glyphs/offsets/lengths, operand_kind=derived_integer, operand_literal=null) which the CMP_MAP gradient hill-climbs into range. This is a genuinely different mechanism from value_precision (which requires a FIXED multi-byte literal operand to converge on -- inapplicable to a computed in-range integer) and from assembly_depth (no size growth). It is FALSIFIABLE+DISCRIMINATING (G1): pure-I2S predicts cmp_min_distance~0; assembly_depth predicts size_lift>=1.3; a fixed-literal mechanism is inapplicable -- only numeric convergence predicts size_lift<1.3 AND vp_tags>=0.3 AND a numeric distance gap. It reuses the BUILT value_distance_reached tool in its numeric/magnitude mode (already a registered capability for inequality gates); the only new param is the per-branch derived-integer cmp-site, supplied from the h1 card. G2-INDEPENDENT: it reads the magnitude reached on the header field in the BLOCKING vs RESOLVING seed sets, not the resolve label.

HONEST decidable:false SUB-GROUP (G3): joint_grammar_state_path covers libxml2/7296, libxml2/7311, libxml2/9361 (automaton non-NULL-state / namespace-count gates resolved via a GRAMMAR-PATH choice -- OPT vs MULT content model, namespaced empty-element close -- a non-local grammar-state property no built tool measures independently of the resolve pattern; depth_reach at the branch line would re-detect vpc-reaches-false-side = G2 violation) and lcms/2674 (a single 4-byte ICC FOURCC at constant size: short-operand chance-alignment prone, already failed both prior rules on real data, no built measurement cleanly separates its subtype). Marked decidable:false on MECHANISM grounds, NOT because libxml2/lcms corpora are off the running server (they are sB-owned with DB seeds) -- per the cross-server rule, design validity is target-agnostic; these are simply non-discriminable with the current built tools. An honest inconclusive is the correct outcome.

DECISION ORDER: assembly_depth (most specific conjunction) first, then value_precision, then numeric_header_convergence (its size_lift<1.3 AND vp_tags>=0.3 conjunction is mutually exclusive with assembly_depth's vp_tags<0.3, so order among them is robust). Anything matching none falls through to inconclusive; the decidable:false grammar-state group never scores a rule (rule:false) and lands in inconclusive by design.
